const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
for f in $(find "$HOME/.codex/sessions" -type f -name '*.jsonl' | tail -n 240); do
 jq -r 'select(.type=="session_meta") | [.payload.id, (.payload.session_id // ""), (.payload.parent_thread_id // ""), (.payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.parent_thread_id // "")] | @tsv' "$f" 2>/dev/null

events.command:
const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$tmpdir"' EXIT
find "$HOME/.codex/sessions" -type f -name '*.jsonl' | while read -r f; do jq -r 'select(.type=="session_meta") | [.payload.id, (.payload.session_id // ""), (.payload.parent_thread_id // ""), (.payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.parent_thread_id // ""), (.payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.depth // "")] | @tsv' "$f" 2>/dev/null; done > "$tmpdir/meta"
cut -f1 "$tmpdir/meta" | sort -u > "$tmpdir/ids"; cut -f2 "$tmpdir/meta" | sed '/^$/d' | sort -u > "$tmpdir/session_ids"; cut -f3 "$tmpdir/meta" | sed '/^$/d' | sort -u > "$tmpdir/parents"
printf 'session_meta_records=%
events.name: exec

finalAssistantMessages.text: The explicit linkage is clear in subagent `session_meta`: the child carries both a top-level `parent_thread_id` and a nested `source.subagent.thread_spawn.parent_thread_id`, plus `depth`. I’m checking the spawn/wait/close call schemas next, especially whether returned IDs can corroborate linkage without becoming the primary grouping key.

A useful caveat surfaced: `session_id` is optional and is not interchangeable with `id` in the sampled metadata. I’m doing one final corpus-wide referential check so the recommendation can distinguish hard edges from merely corroborating spawn-call evidence.

**Observed Schema** Each JSONL record has: - `timestamp` - `type` - `payload` Task identity and ancestry appear in `type: "session_meta"` records. Root/user thread metadata: ```text payload.id: string payload.session_id: string, optional payload.thread_source: "user" payload.source: "vscode" in inspected records ``` Subagent metadata: ```text payload.id: string payload.session_id: string, optional payload.thread_source: "subagent" payload.parent_thread_id: string payload.agent_role: string payload.agent_nickname: string payload.multi_agent_version: string payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.parent_thread_id: string payload.source.subagent.thread_spawn.depth: number payload.source.subagent.t

session.sourceRequest: <recommended_plugins> Here is a list of plugins that are available but not installed. If the user's query would benefit from one of these plugins, use the `request_plugin_install` tool to suggest that they install it. Pass the parenthesized ID as `plugin_id`. For example, suggest the Google Drive plugin if the query could possibly be better answered with access to Google Drive. - Atlassian Rovo (atlassian-rovo@openai-curated-remote) - Box (box@openai-curated-remote) - Gmail (gmail@openai-curated-remote) - Google Calendar (google-calendar@openai-curated-remote) - Google Drive (google-drive@openai-curated-remote) - Notion (notion@openai-curated-remote) - Outlook Calendar (outlook-calendar@openai-curated-remote) - Outlook Email (outlook-email@openai-curated-remote) - SharePoint (sharepoint@openai-curated-remote) - Slack (slack@openai-curated-remote) - Teams (teams@openai-curated-remote) </recommended_plugins> # AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/microwavedev/workspace/microwave-hub <INSTRU
